step2_rag_chunks: If a craving occurs, try changing your environment. Move to another room, go outside for fresh air, or start another activity.

After meals, immediately introduce a competing behavior before the urge has a chance to form â€” get up from the table, brush your teeth, chew sugar-free gum, or take a short walk. The goal is to break the automatic link between finishing a meal and reaching for a cigarette by making a different behavior equally automatic over time.
If you slip and smoke a cigarette, it does not mean you have failed. Many people attempt quitting several times before succeeding.

step2_rag_chunks: Asking a patient to rate their confidence in quitting on a scale of 1 to 10, and then asking what would make it one point higher, is a simple way to identify what support or information they feel they are missing.

When a craving hits, delaying the response by even 5 to 10 minutes is often enough for it to pass. Tell yourself you will smoke in 10 minutes if you still want to â€” and then do something else for those 10 minutes. Most of the time the urge has weakened significantly by the time the 10 minutes are up.
Keeping your hands busy with activities such as drawing, writing, or using a stress ball can help reduce the urge to smoke.

step2_rag_chunks: Having even one person who knows you are trying to quit and supports you makes the process significantly easier. This is not about having someone watch over you â€” it is about having someone to call when the urge is strong, or someone who will not offer you a cigarette. Who in your life could be that person for you?
Talking to someone about your cravings can help reduce the intensity of the urge.
Taking a short walk or doing light physical activity can help reduce stress and distract from cravings.

step2_rag_chunks: When a craving hits, delaying the response by even 5 to 10 minutes is often enough for it to pass. Talking to someone about your cravings can help reduce the intensity of the urge.

A craving is not a command â€” it is a wave. It builds, peaks, and fades, usually within 3 to 5 minutes. Urge surfing means observing the craving without acting on it â€” noticing where you feel it in your body, watching it get stronger, and then watching it pass. Each time you surf an urge without smoking, it gets slightly easier the next time.
